_comment: Under CoALA, each MCP server is an additional GROUNDING action surface
(§4.2). Adding a server extends the external action space; removing one
shrinks it. Treat additions as architectural changes, not config tweaks.
Secrets come from Railway environment variables — never hard-code tokens
here. The ${VAR} syntax is expanded at load time.
- [peers] entries reference a transport key via transport_ref.
- [channels] entries also reference a transport key via transport_ref.
Adding a new platform usually means: an MCP entry here + 1+ peers +
1+ channels in hermes.toml. No code changes required.
Servers below are commented out by default; uncomment and configure the
ones you actually need. Each comes with a note on which CoALA grounding

_filesystem_example._note: GROUNDING — digital. Scoped filesystem access. Built-in tools already cover most of this; enable only if you need explicit MCP-level scoping for subagents.

_postgres_example._note: GROUNDING — digital. Read-only DB introspection. Mark production DBs as read-only in URL; writes require explicit promotion.

_railway_example._note: GROUNDING — digital. If a community Railway MCP is available, mount it here. Otherwise the agent uses the railway CLI via the shell tool.

_brave_search_example._note: GROUNDING — digital. External web search beyond the built-in web tools.

_sentry_example._note: GROUNDING — digital. Error/incident telemetry. Essential for the debug-incident skill if you use Sentry.

_paperclip_ai_example._note: GROUNDING — peer-agent dialogue. Group-agent coordination platform. Replace command/args with the real paperclip_ai MCP server invocation when wiring. Pairs with [[peers.peer]] id='paperclip-ai' and [[channels.channel]] id='paperclip-coord' in hermes.toml.

_multica_example._note: GROUNDING — peer-agent dialogue. Multi-agent swarm platform. Replace command/args with the real multica MCP server invocation when wiring. Pairs with [[peers.peer]] id='multica-swarm' and [[channels.channel]] id='multica-broadcast' in hermes.toml.

_generic_agent_bridge_example._note: GROUNDING — peer-agent dialogue. Protocol-agnostic shape: any group-agent platform that exposes (a) capability discovery, (b) message send, (c) inbound webhook. Use as a template when the platform is NOT MCP-shaped — point at a thin adapter you write yourself that translates MCP calls to the platform's native protocol (HTTP, AT Protocol, ActivityPub, custom).

_disabled_servers_are_ignored: Hermes ignores entries whose key starts with underscore. Rename _foo_example → foo (and drop _disabled / _note) to activate.
